vocab.definition: Proteins that act as catalysts for chemical reactions (speed them up).
The reactant(s) in a chemical reaction that bind to an enzyme.
The place where the substrate binds onto the surface of the enzyme.
The specific sequence of amino acids in a protein chain.
The type of bond that holds amino acids together.
To change the shape of an enzyme (due to pH or temp), which stops it from working.
A polymer made of a folded chain of amino acids.
The monomer (building block) of a protein.
Double-stranded molecule with deoxyribose sugar and bases A, C, G, T.
Single-stranded molecule with ribose sugar and bases A, C, G, U.
The process in the nucleus where mRNA makes a copy of a gene from DNA.
The process in the ribosome where mRNA is used to build a protein.
A sequence of 3 bases that codes for a specific amino acid.
AUG; the codon that signals the ribosome to begin translation.
UAA, UAG, or UGA; codons that signal the end of translation.
Any change in the DNA sequence.
A mutation in a body cell that is NOT passed to offspring.
A mutation in a sex cell (gamete) that CAN be passed to offspring.
A substitution where one base pair is changed to another.
An insertion or deletion that shifts the 'reading frame' of codons.
A chromosomal alteration where part of a chromosome is lost.
When a piece of a chromosome breaks off and reattaches in reverse order.
When a piece of a chromosome attaches to a non-homologous chromosome.
A chromosomal alteration where a section of DNA is repeated.
When chromosomes fail to separate properly during cell division.
An outside agent (like UV rays or chemicals) that causes mutations.
A section of DNA that contains instructions for making a protein.
A different version or variant of the same gene.
The version of a trait that shows up if at least one is present (Capital letter).
The version of a trait that is hidden unless two are present (lowercase letter).
Having two different alleles for a trait (e.g., Bb).
Having two of the same allele for a trait (e.g., BB or bb).
The genetic makeup of an organism (the actual alleles like BB).
The physical appearance or expressed trait of an organism (e.g., Brown hair).
A diagram used to predict the probability of genotypes in offspring.
When two traits blend to create a third phenotype (Red + White = Pink).
When both traits show up together side-by-side (Black + White = Checkered).
When there are more than two possible alleles for a gene (like Blood Type).
Traits controlled by the interaction of multiple genes (like height).
Traits controlled by genes located on the sex chromosomes (usually X).
A chart that shows the inheritance of a trait through generations of a family.
